title: Warren wants Trump’s FTC to see if companies are using tariffs as cover for price gouging Sen. Elizabeth Warren and 15 other Democratic lawmakers are renewing a call for an investigation into whether 'tariff enabled price-gouging' will impact consumers in the years ahead as President Trump's trade moves ripple through the US economy.
CPSC Posts Product Recalls to its Web Site The CPSC announces today the following recalls conducted in cooperation with the firms listed below. Recalls can be viewed at www.cpsc.gov.

Hartford Bakery, Inc. Issues Allergy Alert on Undeclared Hazelnuts in "Lewis Bake Shop Artisan Style 1/2 Loaf" EVANSVILLE, Ind., July 10, 2025--Hartford Bakery, Inc. is voluntarily recalling six lots of its "Lewis Bake Shop Artisan Style 1/2 Loaf" as this product may contain undeclared hazelnuts. People with a nut allergy or severe sensitivity to hazelnuts run the risk of serious or life-threatening allergic reactions if they consume these products.

Crave Hot Dogs & BBQ Launches Inside Louisville Walmart: Southern Flavor Meets Shopping Convenience Crave Hot Dogs & BBQ is bringing a new wave of flavor to Kentucky shoppers with the opening of its express unit inside the Walmart Supercenter at 7101 Cedar Springs Blvd, Louisville, KY 40291. This launch marks a significant milestone in Crave's national expansion and its mission to make the fast-casual BBQ and specialty hot dog chain a household name.

Back-to-school spending set to dip as economic woes shrink budgets, Deloitte forecasts Overall spending is projected to remain flat at $30.9 billion or $570 per student on average, despite higher prices across all product categories, which is 7% below 2021, according to the report.  Last year, parents spent an average of $586 per child.  The Deloitte survey was conducted online using an independent research panel between May 21 and May 30, involving 1,203 parents with at least one child in grades K-12.

Customers seeking deals gave Amazon's Prime Day and competing sales a solid start The first day of Amazon's Prime Day event and competing retail sales that kicked off on Tuesday drove solid online spending compared to a year earlier, according to two data sources.  Adobe Digital Insights, which tracks visits to e-commerce sites, reported that U.S. consumers spent $7.9 billion at online stores on Tuesday, a 9.9% increase from the comparable day last year.  Retailers offered discounts in the range of 9% to 23%, on par with July 2024 sales events, Adobe said.
